meta.name: Pad Thai (ผัดไทย)
meta.subtitle: Classic street-style rice noodles with tamarind, fish sauce, palm sugar, egg, and crunchy toppings.

source.name: SSA Thai Catalog
notes: Deterministic, canonical Pad Thai recipe for SSA catalogs.
Designed for home stovetops with wok OR skillet fallback.
Sauce built on tamarind + fish sauce + palm sugar; lime to finish.
Optional swaps included for allergy/diet scenarios (see variations/adaptations).

summary: A balanced Thai noodle stir-fry: tangy tamarind, savory fish sauce, sweet palm sugar, egg, and bright lime with crunchy peanuts and bean sprouts.

yield.notes: Makes 4 moderate portions or 3 large portions.

notes: Best eaten fresh; leftovers reheat ok with a splash of water.
Sauce can be made ahead; noodles are best cooked day-of.

tasteProfile.notes: Heat is optional; classic Pad Thai is not extremely spicy. Serve chili flakes on the side.

required.label: Wok (preferred) or wide skillet
required.notes: Use a wide skillet if you don’t have a wok; cook in batches to avoid steaming.

optional.label: Mortar & pestle
optional.notes: Nice for lightly crushing peanuts or making a quick chili-lime mix.

ingredients.label: Flat rice noodles (Pad Thai noodles), dried
notes: Use medium-width rice sticks. Thin works, but is easier to overcook.
Soak until pliable; finish cooking in the wok with sauce.

ingredients.label: Tamarind paste/concentrate
ingredients.notes: If using very strong concentrate, start with half and adjust to taste.

ingredients.label: Fish sauce
ingredients.notes: Primary salt/umami. If very strong, reduce slightly.

ingredients.label: Palm sugar (or light brown sugar)
ingredients.notes: Grate or chop palm sugar so it dissolves quickly.

ingredients.label: Light soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Optional booster for color/umami; reduce if salt-sensitive.

ingredients.label: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: This is a baseline. Always finish to taste with fresh lime.

ingredients.label: Hot water (for loosening sauce/noodles as needed)
ingredients.notes: Keep nearby; add in teaspoons to prevent sticking/drying.

ingredients.label: Dried shrimp, chopped (optional but classic)
ingredients.notes: Omit for shellfish allergy or vegetarian. Adds classic savory depth.

ingredients.label: Firm tofu, pressed and diced
ingredients.notes: Classic home version often includes tofu; you can swap chicken/shrimp.

ingredients.label: Eggs
ingredients.notes: Creates the silky coating and richness.

ingredients.label: Bean sprouts, divided
ingredients.notes: Half cooks in the wok; half stays fresh for crunch.

ingredients.label: Garlic chives (or Chinese chives), cut into 2-inch pieces
ingredients.notes: If unavailable, use scallions + a little extra garlic.

ingredients.label: Roasted peanuts, crushed
ingredients.notes: Serve on the side if allergy risk.

ingredients.label: Thai chili flakes (to taste, served on the side)

steps.title: Mix the Pad Thai sauce
instructions: In a bowl, whisk tamarind paste, fish sauce, palm sugar, soy sauce, and lime juice until the sugar is mostly dissolved.
Taste: it should be bold—salty-sour-sweet. Adjust with a little more sugar (sweet), tamarind (sour), or fish sauce (salty) if needed.
Keep nearby. Pad Thai moves fast once the pan is hot.
checks: Sauce tastes slightly too intense on its own (it will mellow on noodles).
Sugar is at least partially dissolved.

steps.title: Soak noodles until pliable
instructions: Place dried rice noodles in a large bowl and cover with warm (not boiling) water.
Soak until flexible but still slightly firm at the center (they will finish in the wok).
Drain well and set aside. If they sit, toss with a teaspoon of oil to prevent sticking.
checks: Center still slightly firm (not fully soft).

steps.title: Prep toppings and aromatics (mise en place)
instructions: Minced garlic; slice shallot; chop dried shrimp if using.
Crush peanuts; slice scallions/garlic chives; portion bean sprouts (half for wok, half for fresh crunch).
Crack eggs into a small bowl and beat lightly.
checks: Everything is within arm’s reach (Pad Thai is a fast stir-fry).

steps.title: Crisp tofu (and/or protein) in hot oil
instructions: Heat wok or wide skillet over high heat until very hot.
Add about 2 tablespoons oil. Add tofu and sear until lightly golden on multiple sides.
Push tofu to the edge (or remove to a plate if your pan is crowded).
checks: Tofu is lightly browned and not watery in the pan.

steps.title: Bloom aromatics (and dried shrimp if using)
instructions: Add a small splash more oil if needed.
Add shallot and garlic; stir-fry 20–30 seconds until fragrant (do not burn).
If using dried shrimp, add and stir for 15–20 seconds to toast.
checks: Garlic is fragrant but not dark brown.

steps.title: Scramble egg in the pan
instructions: Push tofu/aromatics to one side; add a touch of oil to the empty spot.
Pour in beaten eggs and scramble softly.
When just set, mix into the rest.
checks: Egg is set but not dry.

steps.title: Add noodles and sauce; toss until glossy
instructions: Add drained noodles to the pan and pour in the sauce.
Toss continuously with tongs/spatula over high heat so noodles absorb sauce and soften evenly.
If noodles look dry or stiff, add hot water a tablespoon at a time to loosen and help steam-finish them.
Aim for noodles that are tender but still have a little chew.
checks: Noodles are glossy and evenly colored.
Sauce is absorbed; no watery puddle at bottom.
Noodles are tender with slight chew.

steps.title: Finish with sprouts and chives; adjust balance
instructions: Add half the bean sprouts and the garlic chives/scallions. Toss 30–60 seconds just to wilt slightly.
Turn off heat. Taste and adjust: add lime for brightness; add a pinch of sugar if too sour; add a splash fish sauce/soy if flat.
Plate immediately.
checks: Sprouts still have crunch; greens are bright.

steps.title: Serve with toppings (custom heat)
instructions: Top each plate with remaining fresh bean sprouts, crushed peanuts, and cilantro if using.
Serve with lime wedges and chili flakes on the side so everyone controls heat and brightness.

pairings.notes: Pad Thai is rich-sweet-sour; pair with crisp salads or light soups.

leftovers.notes: Reheat with 1–2 tbsp water; toss quickly to loosen.

fridge.notes: Store noodles airtight. Add fresh sprouts/peanuts at serving time, not storage time.

notes: Cook eggs fully to your safety preference.
If using shrimp (fresh), cook to opaque and firm; avoid cross-contamination with other foods.

adaptations.title: No wok available
notes: Use a wide skillet, get it very hot, and cook in batches to avoid steaming.
If crowded, remove tofu/protein before tossing noodles; add back at the end.

adaptations.title: Low-smoke indoor cooking
notes: Preheat pan well but avoid extreme smoking oil; use avocado oil if available.
Cook protein first, remove, then finish noodles quickly.

ingredient.tofu_firm.notes: Slice thin; sear quickly over high heat.

ingredient.shrimp.label: Shrimp, peeled and deveined
ingredient.shrimp.notes: Sear 60–90 seconds per side; remove before noodles; add back at end.

variations.label: Vegetarian (no fish sauce / no dried shrimp)

ingredient.fish_sauce.notes: Add salt to taste; keep lime/tamarind for brightness.

ingredient.shrimp_dried.notes: Skip. Add extra shallot/garlic and a pinch of mushroom seasoning.

ingredient.fish_sauce.notes: Thin miso with hot water; add late to avoid scorching.

ingredient.egg.notes: Skip egg. Optionally add extra tofu and finish with more lime.

ingredient.peanuts.notes: Use toasted sesame or pumpkin seeds for crunch.

notes: Key to great Pad Thai at home: soak noodles (don’t boil), very hot pan, and keep things moving.
If your tamarind is very sour/strong, reduce and balance with sugar and lime.
Serve chili flakes separately so each person controls heat.

reviewNotes: All ingredients include id+label and amount with value/unit.
All steps include id+title and instructions[].
